ksa_text: Knowledge of human-AI oversight architecture, including human-in-the-loop and human-on-the-loop design patterns, autonomy level frameworks, override and intervention mechanisms, and the governance structures required to maintain meaningful human control over AI system decisions.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of intelligence capabilities and limitations.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA AI-K-029 (AI & ML/Knowledge) scores 0.507 composite (highest: NIST AI RMF at 0.507). Scope is edge_case for AI Content Editor: 0 in-scope signals, 0 out-of-scope signals. Seniority is appropriate.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Include consistent with edge_case scope, AI & ML, composite 0.507. Decision stands.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence support AI & ML for AI Content Editor?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: AI & ML appears in approximately 62% of AI Content Editor job postings. AI system understanding and prompt engineering in ~60% of AI editor JDs.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Include upheld. AI & ML is market-validated for AI Content Editor. Final decision: include.
